summary: Policy-backed control for Storage Sense automatic cleanup. Useful when users want manual control over disk cleanup, but it removes an automatic housekeeping feature that helps many casual users.

setting.name: Storage Sense Automatic Cleanup
setting.casual_explanation: Storage Sense is Windows' automatic cleanup feature for temporary and unneeded files. This policy decides whether the feature is allowed at all on the device.
setting.why_people_change_it: Some users disable it because they want full manual control over cleanup and do not want Windows deleting items automatically. Others keep it on because it helps recover space with less effort.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling Storage Sense gives you more manual control, but Windows stops running its automatic cleanup flow.
setting.professional_notes: The policy writes AllowStorageSenseGlobal under the StorageSense policy key. StorageSense.admx uses enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows policy does not force Storage Sense and normal Windows behavior applies.

allowed_values.label: Storage Sense disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Storage Sense is blocked by policy.

allowed_values.label: Storage Sense enabled
allowed_values.meaning: Storage Sense is explicitly allowed by policy.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes AllowStorageSenseGlobal = 0, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default

states.rationale: Policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: This record tracks the policy baseline, not the user-facing Settings default.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who want Windows to manage storage cleanup normally
avoid_for: Users who want strict manual cleanup control

states.rationale: Keep the device at the default policy baseline.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want normal Windows cleanup behavior and do not need to force a policy decision.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to guarantee that Storage Sense will not run automatically.
tradeoffs: Windows stays free to manage cleanup normally.
No hard policy lock is applied.

recommended_profiles.label: Manual cleanup only
intended_for: People who want manual disk cleanup control
avoid_for: Casual users who benefit from automatic cleanup
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to stop running automatic cleanup in the background.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You rely on Storage Sense to reclaim space automatically.
tradeoffs: You need to manage cleanup yourself.

evidence.title: Microsoft Storage Policy CSP
evidence.summary: Official documentation for AllowStorageSenseGlobal and related Storage Sense policies.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft StorageSense.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file shows enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 for AllowStorageSenseGlobal.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes AllowStorageSenseGlobal = 0 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak provenance record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Local Microsoft Storage Sense policy files document the exact policy surface, value semantics, and default behavior. The app writes the documented disable value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: StorageSense.admx: key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\StorageSense" valueName="AllowStorageSenseGlobal"; StorageSense.adml: Disabled: Storage Sense is turned off the machine. Users cannot enable Storage Sense.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact key and value name; local ADML confirms that the disabled policy state turns Storage Sense off for the machine.
